purpose: Map each row in the README MLX-inference performance table to a candidate GGUF + llama.cpp Metal architecture for shape-compatible external baseline benchmarking.
claim_boundary: Rows produced from this manifest are shape-compatible external GGUF baselines only. llama-bench does not consume the ax/MLX prompt-token JSON, so the resulting numbers are NOT prompt-hash parity evidence and must NEVER be merged into the repo-owned MLX prefill/decode/TTFT tables. They belong in a separate 'External GGUF baseline' section with explicit disclaimer.
quant_mapping_policy: MLX bit-width is mapped to the closest standard bartowski GGUF K-quant: 4-bit -> Q4_K_M, 5-bit -> Q5_K_M, 6-bit -> Q6_K, 8-bit -> Q8_0. bartowski is the required GGUF publisher for this inventory so llama.cpp rows stay on standard imatrix K-quants instead of Unsloth Dynamic UD variants. Architectural bit-for-bit equivalence is NOT claimed.
resolution_policy: Each row lists candidate (repo, filename_pattern) pairs in priority order. The sweep driver resolves the first candidate whose pattern matches a file via huggingface_hub.list_repo_files. Status 'candidate' means unresolved; the driver flips it to 'resolved' or 'unresolved' at sweep time. Rows where llama.cpp architecture support is unknown stay 'candidate' until the driver attempts a load.

rows.multimodal_note: Source MLX is multimodal (Gemma4 + audio). GGUF candidates must be text-only conversions.

rows.prompt_source_note: Prompt-token source is mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit. GGUF side benches the bartowski standard Q4_K_M imatrix quant. Injected into the README '4-bit' row as a shape-compatible reference only.
